name: 14K Gold & Coin
description_short: Locally-owned pawn shop in Ypsilanti, MI buying and selling jewelry, coins, electronics, and tools with over 15 years of experience. Offers instant cash transactions on gold, diamonds, and various merchandise.
description_long: 14K Gold & Coin is a family-owned pawn shop located at 1518 Wastenaw Avenue in Ypsilanti, Michigan, operating for over 15 years in the community. The business specializes in buying and selling precious metals, jewelry, coins, and a wide variety of consumer goods. Their core business model centers on purchasing valuables from customers for immediate cash while also maintaining inventory of previously-owned items for resale. The company positions itself as a local alternative to national chains, emphasizing personalized service and fair valuations. The shop maintains both a physical retail location and online presence through eBay and Facebook Marketplace storefronts, extending their reach beyond walk-in traffic. 14K Gold & Coin offers evaluation and cash purchase services for jewelry including men's and women's pieces, wedding bands, diamonds, gemstones, dental gold, and class rings. Beyond precious metals, they buy and sell electronics (laptops, phones, computers, flat-screen TVs, cameras), power tools from major brands (Milwaukee, DeWalt, Ryobi, Bosch, Ridgid), and musical instruments. The company emphasizes confidentiality and speed in transactions, advertising "instant cash on the spot" with convenient handling. What distinguishes 14K Gold & Coin is their stated guarantee that they "pay more" than competitors, their 15+ years of local operational history, and their family-ownership model in a market typically dominated by corporate chains. The shop has generated 309 customer ratings with a 4.6-star average, suggesting consistent positive customer experiences. Their multi-category inventory approach (jewelry, coins, electronics, tools) differentiates them from shops focused exclusively on precious metals. An honest assessment reveals that pawn transactions inherently involve selling items for below retail value, and customers should understand they will receive less than original purchase prices. The website lacks specific information about loan offerings, interest rates, or terms if they provide pawn loans rather than purchase-only transactions. No details are provided about appraisal methodologies, certification processes, or authentication standards for high-value items like diamonds and gemstones.
pros: 4.6-star rating across 309 customer reviews indicates consistent positive service experiences
Over 15 years of local operating history provides established track record and community presence
Family-owned business structure suggests personalized customer service versus corporate chain model
Wide merchandise categories (jewelry, coins, electronics, tools) provide one-stop shopping convenience
Online sales channels through eBay and Facebook Marketplace extend purchasing options beyond physical store
Clear inventory in major power tool brands (DeWalt, Milwaukee, Ryobi, Bosch) with stated 'largest selection' in area
Advertises fast, confidential transactions with instant cash payment—major advantage for urgent liquidity needs
cons: Pawn shop model inherently means selling items for substantially below original retail prices
Website provides no details about jewelry appraisal methodology, certification standards, or authentication processes for diamonds and gemstones
No information disclosed about whether business offers pawn loans versus purchase-only transactions, or any loan terms/interest rates if applicable
Limited operational hours (closes at 6pm weekdays, 4pm Saturday, closed Sunday) may not accommodate all customer schedules
Website lacks customer testimonial details or specific case examples of valuations to help shoppers gauge fair pricing expectations
best_for: Individuals needing immediate cash for jewelry, coins, electronics, or tools who prioritize speed over maximum resale value
Local Ypsilanti residents seeking personalized service from family-owned businesses rather than corporate pawn chains
Buyers searching for previously-owned tools, electronics, and jewelry with physical inspection before purchase
Sellers of bulk merchandise (estates, tool collections, jewelry lots) seeking one-visit liquidation with cash payment
diagnosis: Best for customers in the Ypsilanti area needing fast cash for jewelry, tools, or electronics who prioritize speed and local service over maximum resale value. Primary caveat: pawn transactions yield substantially less than retail prices, and customers should not expect original purchase value; the website provides minimal transparency on appraisal methods or authentication standards for high-value items.
services: Gold, platinum, and diamond jewelry evaluation and purchase
Wedding band and engagement ring buying
Electronics buying (laptops, phones, computers, flat-screen TVs, cameras)
Power tool purchasing (DeWalt, Milwaukee, Ryobi, Bosch, Ridgid brands)
